summary: Russian forces are increasingly deploying "Molnias" (lightning drones) and strike/reconnaissance drones, but their control frequencies remain difficult to disrupt due to their one-way signal reception. Shahed-136 and Shahed-131 drone attacks on Sumy and other cities have sharply declined in recent weeks, likely due to production issues, intensified electronic warfare, and the loss of Starlink connectivity. The "Rubikon" Center, a Russian unmanned systems unit, reported destroying 114 Ukrainian targets on March 9 and 162 on March 10, including personnel and fixed-wing UAVs, while recruiting operators and specialists with a 2.3 million ₽ signing bonus. Ukrainian forces continue to use FPV drones effectively, as seen in an attack where two drones simultaneously struck a Russian soldier behind cover.
